text.title: Domain and Handle Availability Checking
text.summary: Priority order for domain and handle checks, actions by availability status, alternative strategies when primary is taken, and agent-specific gotchas.

rule.statement: Check .com first. If .com is taken by an active product, move to the next name candidate rather than accepting an alternative TLD for a serious brand.
rule.rationale: .com is still the default for credibility in B2B. .io is acceptable for devtools. .ai only if ML product and budget tolerates premium. Skip .xyz/.tech for serious brands.

list.item: Priority 6 — LinkedIn company URL slug (nice to have)
Also check: npm package, PyPI package if applicable

rule.statement: Register handles on all platforms in the same hour as registering the domain — even if unused — to prevent squatting.
rule.rationale: GitHub org, npm/PyPI, Twitter, LinkedIn — squatters monitor new domain registrations and claim matching handles within hours.

list.item: Premium (priced) → Consider if under $5K for revenue-generating product
Taken (parked >5 years, price >$5K) → Walk away for non-revenue projects
Taken (active product) → Move to next name candidate

section.title: Alternative Domain Strategies
section.p: When primary .com is taken, try in order before accepting an alternative TLD:

list.item: Creative spelling (only if still unambiguous): taskflw.com

section.reference: Domain/handle availability matrix template with trademark check and fallback alternatives.
Bulk availability runner: reads names.txt, outputs CSV matrix across .com/.io/.co, GitHub, npm, PyPI, Twitter.

rule.statement: Trademark check must end at a human attorney. Agent USPTO TESS output is an informational risk band, not legal clearance.
rule.rationale: Agents miss similar marks (phonetic, design). A free .com that infringes is worse than a paid premium that is clear.

antipattern.description: Treating agent trademark pre-filter as legal clearance.
antipattern.reason: USPTO TESS HTML changes yearly, scrapes break, and phonetic similarity is not caught by string matching. Agent output is risk-band only.

antipattern.description: WHOIS responses vary by registry — "no match" / "not found" / "no entries" / "AVAILABLE" are all different strings; agent regex misses some.
antipattern.reason: Some TLDs deny WHOIS to non-authenticated clients (.de needs DENIC TCP); agents falsely report "available". Always confirm at registrar API, not just WHOIS.

antipattern.description: Price scraping stale domains without re-fetching within 48h of registration decision.
antipattern.reason: Premium domain pricing fluctuates; scraped prices can be weeks old and the listed price may have changed or the domain may have been claimed.

example.note: TaskFlow availability: primary taken, fallback found
